text.title: Legal Compliance Checklist — Agent Rules
text.summary: Safety rules for agents running compliance audits: presence vs accuracy distinction, cookie-banner UX traps, DSAR handling.

rule.statement: This checklist verifies presence and process, not legal sufficiency. "Privacy policy exists" is NOT the same as "privacy policy is accurate and legally compliant." Always distinguish between presence checks (automated) and content accuracy checks (require attorney review).
rule.rationale: Agents frequently conflate "page exists" with "compliant." A privacy policy that says "we do not collect data" for a product that collects extensive data is worse than having no policy — it is an affirmative false statement.

rule.statement: Do not auto-publish any policy draft. All policy changes require: redline (diff vs prior version), human + counsel review gate, approval log entry, and then publication with effective-date update.
rule.rationale: Policy edits that bypass counsel review and change log create litigation risk and hide changes from the legal record.

rule.statement: Audit cookie consent in both EU and non-EU locales. EU requires opt-in (pre-ticked boxes prohibited, reject must be as easy as accept). CCPA requires opt-out. Do not assume one pattern satisfies both.
rule.rationale: Cookie scanners running against default (US) locale miss EU-specific requirements. Re-run audits with EU IP or locale headers to check the compliant path.

rule.statement: When auditing cookie compliance, also run after user login. Many analytics trackers activate only for authenticated users and are missed by logged-out scans.
rule.rationale: Authenticated-only trackers (e.g. session replay, product analytics with user IDs) are frequently the highest-risk data flows and the ones most often missed by standard cookie audits.

rule.statement: Data Subject Access Requests (right to access, correct, delete, export) must be routed to a human review queue. Never auto-respond to DSAR emails. Never auto-execute data deletion without identity verification.
rule.rationale: Auto-responding to a deletion request without verifying identity could delete the wrong user's data. Identity verification requirements are specified in GDPR and CCPA and cannot be satisfied by email alone.

section.title: Prompt patterns

example.note: Site audit prompt: "Fetch [url]/privacy and [url]/terms. Parse and return: last-updated date, list of third parties named, list of user rights stated, presence/absence of: GDPR DPO contact, CCPA Do Not Sell link, CAN-SPAM physical address. Output as JSON. Note: this verifies PRESENCE only, not accuracy or legal sufficiency."

section.reference: Headless legal-page presence audit with EU cookie-banner check
